what_i_noticed: In stock_analyzer.py, the Ito correction at line 169 references garch_vol BEFORE it is assigned at line 178 (garch_vol = None). Because Python treats garch_vol as local (due to the later assignment), this raises UnboundLocalError at runtime, which is silently caught by the broad try/except at line 262. This means analyze_stock() returns None for EVERY stock, completely breaking /api/stock/{ticker} and /api/stock/screener endpoints.
hypothesis: Moving the GARCH fitting block (lines 178-205) before the Ito correction (lines 162-170) will fix the UnboundLocalError, making analyze_stock() functional again. This restores all per-stock MC projections with GARCH vol, block bootstrap, and correct Ito correction.
what_i_did: Reordered stock_analyzer.py: moved GARCH fitting and historical residual computation BEFORE the Ito correction block. This ensures garch_vol is computed (or set to None) before it's referenced in the Ito sigma selection. No logic changes, pure ordering fix.

before.analyze_stock_returns: None for ALL stocks (UnboundLocalError silently caught)

after.analyze_stock_returns: Full analysis dict for all stocks

analysis: This is a critical production bug fix. The Ito correction added in cycle 15 introduced a variable ordering error: garch_vol was referenced at line 169 but assigned at line 178. Python's scoping rules make garch_vol local to the function (because of the assignment at 178), so line 169 raises UnboundLocalError every time. The broad try/except at line 262 silently catches this, causing analyze_stock() to return None for every stock. This means: (1) /api/stock/{ticker} returns HTTP 500 or empty, (2) /api/stock/screener returns an empty list, (3) all GARCH, block bootstrap, and Ito correction improvements from cycles 3-15 were not actually being applied to stock analysis. The data_generator's drift metrics were unaffected because it runs its own independent simple GBM (not the backend's analyze_stock). The fix is trivial but high-impact: reorder the code so GARCH fitting happens before the Ito correction.
next_steps: 1. Add a fast unit test that calls analyze_stock with a mock ticker to catch ordering/scoping bugs. 2. Consider narrowing the broad try/except in analyze_stock to not silently swallow all errors. 3. Wire return_model predictions into stock_analyzer (ml_predicted_return parameter). 4. Per-sector GARCH fits on sector ETF returns.
